name: Illinois Dietitian License
description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Illinois - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Dietetic and Nutrition Services Practice Board
provider.parentOrganization: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)

address.streetAddress:
IDFPR Chicago Office
555 West Monroe Street, 5th Floor
Chicago, IL 60661
address.description:
Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation
320 W. Washington Street, 3rd Floor
Springfield, IL 62786

requiredDocuments.name: Official College Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Degree-granting institution transcripts must be sent directly to the IDFPR or uploaded securely through the CORE portal.

requiredDocuments.name: Proof of Examination
requiredDocuments.description: Primary source verification of your CDR exam score, typically routed electronically by the credentialing agency.

requiredDocuments.name: Verification of Supervised Practice
requiredDocuments.description: Documentation proving the completion of the mandatory 900-hour supervised practice requirement.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: What is the fee for an Illinois LDN license?

acceptedAnswer.text: The IDFPR requires a flat $100 fee for the initial application.

mainEntity.name: When does the Illinois LDN license expire?

acceptedAnswer.text: All individual dietitian nutritionist licenses expire simultaneously on October 31 of every odd-numbered year.

mainEntity.name: How many continuing education hours are required to renew my IL license?

acceptedAnswer.text: You must complete 30 hours of continuing education (CE) during each two-year renewal cycle prior to the October 31 expiration date.

mainEntity.name: Does Illinois have reciprocity for dietitians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Illinois. To work in Illinois, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Illinois?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Illinois while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
